mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.4pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% month-over-month during a monetary tightening cycle confirms robust supply-side expansion that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening cycles, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures reflect strengthening household purchasing power that immediately lifts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with rising RSAFS, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 per month indicates a healthy labor market that supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative when payroll growth exceeds 150,000, or the signal fails to predict returns during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings above 3.5% year-over-year force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production signal weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling windows.
